The Discipline

Elements of Mastery.

Every variable is considered. From the exact temperature of the rice to the specific day a fish reaches its peak umami. True luxury is found in the invisible details.

01

Shari (The Rice)

Sourced exclusively from a single family farm in Niigata. Cooked in a traditional iron hagama and seasoned with our proprietary blend of three aged red vinegars (akazu). Served precisely at body temperature.
02

Neta (The Catch)

Flown in daily from Toyosu Market and trusted local spearfishermen. Each piece dictates its own preparation—some served immediately for texture, others aged for days in a controlled environment to maximize umami.
03

Nikiri (The Glaze)

Our house soy glaze is simmered with roasted sake, mirin, and kombu, creating a delicate balance that enhances rather than masks the natural profile of the fish. Applied with a singular brushstroke.

The Experience

A pure, meditative dialogue
between chef and guest.

Every detail considered.
Every moment intentional.

An absolute masterclass
in subtlety.

The Culinary Review

Offerings

The Omakase Paths.

We offer two distinct journeys. Both require a surrender to the chef's daily selections, guided entirely by what the ocean provides on that specific morning.

Beverage pairings curated by our in-house sommelier are available upon seating.

Daylight Journey

$250 / Guest
15 Pieces 90 Minutes Lunch Only

A focused progression of pure nigiri. Highlighting the day's freshest arrivals from Toyosu. Concludes with our signature tamago and clear soup.

Signature Evening

$400 / Guest
22 Courses 150 Minutes Dinner Only

The complete expression of Kinzan. Begins with 6 intricate seasonal otsumami (appetizers) showcasing varied techniques, followed by a masterful 16-piece nigiri sequence.

Reservations

Secure a Seat.

Due to the intimate nature of our 8-seat counter, general reservations are released on the 1st of each month. For private buyouts or special inquiries, please connect with us directly.

Philosophy

Time as an Ingredient.

Our journey began not with a desire to add, but to strip away. Chef Kenji spent two decades in Tokyo mastering the invisible variables of sushi: the meticulous aging of fish to break down proteins into pure umami, the exact acidity curve of the rice, the resting time of the neta before it meets the shari.

Kinzan is the physical culmination of this obsessive study. A space crafted from raw hinoki wood and textured stone—an intimate theater where time slows down, allowing the subtle nuances of each season to be tasted with absolute, quiet clarity.

Kenji Nakamura

Head Chef & Founder

K I N Z A N

An intimate omakase experience dedicated to the mastery of Edo-mae sushi, the rhythm of the seasons, and the art of stillness.
